# CSP Overview in Astro 6

## What is CSP in Astro 6?

Content Security Policy (CSP) is a **stable** feature in Astro 6 (released as experimental in earlier versions). It minimizes XSS attack vectors by controlling which resources a page is allowed to load.

## How Astro Implements CSP

When enabled, Astro:

1. Computes SHA hashes for ALL bundled scripts and styles at build time
2. Injects a `<meta http-equiv="content-security-policy">` in each page's `<head>`
3. The `content` attribute includes `script-src` and `style-src` directives with generated hashes

```html
<!-- Generated by Astro build -->
<meta
  http-equiv="content-security-policy"
  content="script-src 'sha512-abc123...' 'sha512-def456...'; style-src 'sha512-ghi789...';"
>
```

## What Astro CSP Covers

| Resource | Covered |
|----------|---------|
| Bundled scripts | Yes — auto-hashed |
| Inline styles in `.astro` | Yes — auto-hashed |
| CSS Modules | Yes — auto-hashed |
| External scripts (CDN) | No — must add hash manually |
| External stylesheets | No — must add hash manually |
| Shiki code blocks | No — uses inline styles |

## Important Limitations

- **Dev mode**: CSP is inactive during `astro dev` — test with `astro build && astro preview`
- **ViewTransitions**: `<ClientRouter />` is incompatible — use native View Transition API
- **Shiki**: Inline styles in code blocks are not supported
- **External resources**: Must compute and add SHA hashes manually

## Build + Preview Testing

```bash
# Always test CSP with production build
astro build && astro preview
```
